publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

p.subject: {**Subject Code, Use collection subject browse list}

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2021-08-30 18:09:04 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Academic and professional papers, including correspondence, material on technical societies, records of his consulting work, and collected subject files. Includes information on his textbook Unit Operations of Chemical Engineering also indices of Ithaca Journal obituaries. Includes an extensive collection of daily diaries from throughout Smith's life and several scrapbooks. Other material includes family diplomas and correspondence related to his time spent working on the Manhattan Project.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Professor Emeritus of Chemical Engineering, Cornell University.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Academic and professional papers, including correspondence, material on technical societies, records of his consulting work, and collected subject files. Includes information on his textbook Unit Operations of Chemical Engineering also indices of Ithaca Journal obituaries. Includes an extensive collection of daily diaries from throughout Smith's life and several scrapbooks. Other material includes family diplomas and correspondence related to his time spent working on the Manhattan Project.
Also, The School of Chemical Engineering at Cornell: A History of the First Fifty Years; Breaking Ninety, A History of the Country Club of Ithaca, 1900-1990; and "Family History: Julian C. Smith."

arrangement.head: SERIES LIST
arrangement.p: Series III. Publications & Related Materials Boxes 10, 14, 18
Series IV. Teaching & Cornell University Boxes 2-4, 9-11, 13

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Julian C. Smith Papers, #16-7-2474. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

accessrestrict.head: Access Restrictions:
accessrestrict.p: Access to diaries (1947-2015) in Boxes 19-21 are restricted until 2046 or by permission of the family.

controlaccess.subject: Chemical engineering -- Study and teaching.
Country clubs -- New York (State) -- Ithaca.
controlaccess.corpname: Cornell University. College of Engineering -- : History.
Cornell University. School of Chemical Engineering

did.unittitle: Consulting and other Professional Income & Expense Ledger

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes programs, photos, newspaper clippings, Alpha Delta Phi materials, telegrams, correspondence, The Cornell Engineer Vol. 5 No. 1, Cornell Daily Sun, golf score cards, Wendell Willkie for President buttons, Phi Kappa Phi materials, pencil sketches, rejection note from The New Yorker

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes church service programs, newspaper clippings, photographs, cards, lyric sheets, concert programs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Given "In honor of his term as Director, School of Chemical Engineering, Cornell University 1975-1983"

scopecontent.p: Includes sketches, family photographs, Bertha Louise Alexander's will, Joslyn A. Smith obituaries, Smith family genealogy

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes two photos, of Louise and Joslyn Smith on the right and most likely Bertha Smith on the right

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photos of Milan, Paris, United Kingdom

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Front label: "Howard J + Julie / Beautiful western Black n White Photos / Ansel Adams"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photos of Morocco, Tangier, Canada, Germany, France, Bermuda, Europe, loose photo of Julian's sister, Florence Smith and Stefanie Warden in 1985

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photos of French, Italy, Turkey, Syria, Lebanon, Israel, Egypt, Iraq, Jordan, New York, Japan

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes note from Ed King '41 identifying everyone in the photo

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Canadian newspapers with headlines from King George VI's coronation, German and Japanese surrenders in World War II

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "This map with the data shown thereon was prepared during the years 1936, 1937, & 1938 by Julian Cleveland Smith. M.E., E.E., LL.D., Member E.I.C. Assisting in this work were J. Napolean Longval. C.E., and Alberta A. Heath Jr. A novel type supersonic sounding machine designed and built by Julian Cleveland Smith M.E., E.E., LL.D. and Arthur S. Runciman, E.E., Assoc. member E.I.C. was largely responsible for the successful completion of this work"

scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence, artciles 45th Reunion materials, sketches
